description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a dark, rich wood grain finish. The front face displays a large, black textured speaker grille, flanked below by two prominent round knobs and a horizontal tuning scale with a cream background and frequency markings. A small metallic nameplate is centered above the tuning scale, adding a touch of classic detail. The rear panel includes two circular ports, a vented section, several control dials, and a power switch, indicating its functional components. The sides and top are smooth, uninterrupted wood, emphasizing the retro aesthetic and solid construction. This radio receiver would be a striking feature in an urban café, vintage shop, or community center, blending nostalgia with utility.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a black speaker grille, two control knobs, and a horizontal tuning scale, all set in a dark wood cabinet. The rear features ports, controls, and a power switch.
description_view_0: Front view shows the black speaker grille, two round knobs, a horizontal tuning scale, and a small metallic nameplate.
description_view_1: Side view reveals a smooth, uninterrupted dark wood surface with rounded edges and no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view displays two circular ports, a vented section, several control dials, and a power switch, all set in the wooden back panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the other side, showing only the smooth, dark wood finish with no additional features.
